description_long: This is a large, rounded ceramic vase with a wide, open mouth and a bulbous body that tapers gently toward the base. The surface is matte and displays a weathered, mottled appearance with earthy tones of gray, beige, and faint green, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. Subtle surface irregularities and visible cracks indicate it has endured some wear over time. The rim is slightly uneven, and the body lacks any decorative embellishments or handles, giving it a utilitarian, rustic character. The interior appears dark and unglazed, consistent with traditional hand-crafted pottery. This vase would be well-suited for outdoor or garden settings, blending naturally with urban or historical environments.
description: A large, weathered ceramic vase with a wide mouth, rounded body, and a matte, mottled surface in earthy tones. It shows visible cracks and surface wear, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: The vase is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting its wide mouth and the mottled, weathered surface with some discoloration near the rim.
description_view_1: This view emphasizes the rounded body and shows a more uniform surface coloration, with subtle patches and a slightly uneven rim.
description_view_2: A prominent crack is visible running vertically along the body, and the surface appears smoother with less discoloration compared to other views.
description_view_3: The vase appears more uniformly colored with minor surface marks, and the curvature of the body is clearly visible.

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
